string; serialize them with make_yaml() when the yaml module is

produce empty CSV fields as before.

commit.message:
Fix CSS cascade layers and tools filter dark mode

Wrap global base styles (body, headings) in @layer base and
component styles (layout, prose, transitions) in @layer components
so Tailwind utilities can properly override them via the cascade.
Previously, unlayered CSS always beat layered Tailwind utilities
regardless of specificity, causing dark mode text classes to fail.

Also fix the tools category filter JS to set complete className
strings with dark: variants instead of toggling individual classes
that lacked dark mode support.

fix: populate client form fields correctly on edit page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui-server): clear full require.cache on file change for SSR (#1050) (#1092)

The watcher previously only cleared require.cache entries matching
srcDir or entryPath prefixes. Files outside these paths (e.g., generated
files, shared lib/ directories) stayed cached, causing SSR to serve
stale content after edits.

Now clears the entire require.cache on any file change, ensuring all
transitive dependencies are re-evaluated. Performance cost is negligible
since Bun re-evaluates modules fast.

Extracted clearSSRRequireCache() as a testable, exported function.

Closes #1050

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ios): crash on launch — GoogleService-Info.plist missing from Xcode project (#110)

* fix(ios): add GoogleService-Info.plist to Xcode project to fix launch crash

GoogleService-Info.plist existed on disk but was never registered in
project.pbxproj, so it was not copied into the app bundle.
FirebaseApp.configure() in AppDelegate could not find it and called
fatalError(), crashing the app immediately on launch.

Changes:
- Register GoogleService-Info.plist in PBXFileReference, PBXBuildFile,
  PBXGroup, and PBXResourcesBuildPhase so it is bundled with the app
- Register App.entitlements in the project and add CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS
  build setting (Debug + Release) so Associated Domains work
- Add safety check in setup-native.sh that warns if GoogleService-Info.plist
  is missing from the Xcode project
- Bump version to 1.0.7 (build 10007)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ios): fail build if GoogleService-Info.plist is missing from Xcode project

Better to catch this at build time than discover it as a crash in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: commit extension package-lock.json for reproducible builds

Nix only provides a dev shell (nodejs, rollup) but does not pin npm
dependency versions. Without the lockfile, npm install resolves caret
ranges fresh each time, making builds non-reproducible.

CI: Catch all sqllogic test failures and resolve test_datasets permissions (#418)

* FIX Change premissions to resolve permission denied error

In 'Run sqllogic tests' we see a permissions denied error from some of the tests, this should fix the IO error

* FIX Catch all sqllogic test failures, not just GPU errors

The previous check only detected GPU execution errors via grep, missing
other test failures. The tee pipe also masked the test binary's exit code,
causing failures to silently pass. Now captures PIPESTATUS to detect
non-zero exits and expands the grep to also match FAILURES SUMMARY.

commit.message:
fix(vertz): use meta-package in scaffolded apps + add missing subpath exports (#1089)

* fix(vertz): add missing subpath exports and use meta-package in scaffolded apps

- Add `vertz/db/sqlite`, `vertz/ui-server/bun-plugin`, `vertz/theme-shadcn`
  subpath exports to the `vertz` meta-package
- Update scaffolded app template to use `vertz` as single dependency
  instead of individual `@vertz/*` packages
- Update compiler's `isFromImport()` to recognize `vertz/*` imports
  alongside `@vertz/*` so codegen works with meta-package imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(vertz): keep theme-shadcn as separate dep, not in meta-package

Themes are an explicit user choice with real runtime weight (~160KB),
not framework infrastructure. If dozens of themes exist, bundling all
in the meta-package would bloat every install. The scaffolded app now
lists `@vertz/theme-shadcn` as a separate dependency alongside `vertz`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pipeline first-stage: 3 bugs causing 0 candidates from 27,632 objects

Bug 1 — UnboundLocalError: boost_factor
  boost_factor was assigned only in the sigma<5 branch but referenced
  unconditionally after; this raised UnboundLocalError for every object
  with sigma≥5, caught by outer except: continue → 0 candidates survived.
  Fix: assign boost_factor=1.0 in the sigma≥5 branch.

Bug 2 — Placeholder orbital elements trigger sigma-5 instability
  CAD objects get e=0.1, i=10.0, a=1.0 as placeholder orbital elements.
  Running the sigma-5 detector on them produces log(0) → sigma=11+ for
  every object, flagging all 27,632 as artificial.
  Fix: _detect_artificial_neo() returns None when e==0.1 and i==10.0 and
  a==1.0 (the known placeholder triplet).

Bug 3 — _simple_first_stage_scoring ignores encounter geometry
  The fallback scorer only used orbital elements (all hardcoded), so
  inclination=10.0<30 gave score=0.3 to every object unconditionally.
  Fix: when miss_distance_au is present (CAD path), score = exp(-dist/0.02)
  → threshold at ~0.05 AU; slow velocity (< 5 km/s) adds 0.15 bonus.
  Result: only close approaches < 0.05 AU pass the 0.08 first-stage gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hearth: Live Activity group expansion/scrolling broken (Forge-0z2) (#156)

* fix(hearth): Live Activity panel scrolling and group expansion

Replace broken activityScroll integer with the shared scrollViewport
component used by all other panels. Activity groups can now be expanded
(Enter on collapsed header) and collapsed (Esc on expanded lines).
Display order is newest-first with proper viewport clamping that
prevents content overlap.

Bead: Forge-0z2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-0z2

- Lazily initialize activityExpanded map before writes in Enter/Esc
  handlers to prevent nil-map panic on zero-value Model construction
- Track previously selected worker ID in UpdateWorkersMsg and call
  resetActivityState() when ClampToTotal implicitly shifts the cursor
  to a different worker, preventing stale expansion/cursor state
- Update renderWorkerActivity docstring to accurately describe Enter
  expanding and Esc collapsing (not Enter toggling both)
- Update Enter handler comment to clarify it only expands groups

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ghpr): use GraphQL for pending review requests to detect Copilot

The gh CLI's --json reviewRequests field does not serialize Bot-type
reviewers (like copilot-pull-request-reviewer), causing Bellows to
think there are no pending reviews and showing the PR as Ready to Merge
while Copilot review is still pending.

CheckStatus now fetches review requests via GraphQL which correctly
returns all reviewer types (User, Team, Bot, Mannequin), falling back
to the gh CLI data if the GraphQL call fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add title filter to class action search — remove noise from body-text matches

The API full-text search returns thousands of cases that merely *mention*
"class action" in passing (criminal, family, labour cases). Now requires
the TITLE to contain a class action keyword (English + French) to qualify.

Also increased per-query cap to 10K to ensure recent cases aren't missed
(API sorts by relevance, not date).
